MPIPE_PDESC_t Union Reference
[mPIPE Device API (gxio/mpipe.h)]

MPIPE Packet Descriptor. More...

 Written by the classification program.

uint_reg_t words [8]
 Word access.

Detailed Description

MPIPE Packet Descriptor.

The packet descriptor is filled by the mPIPE's classification, load-balancing, and buffer management services. Some fields are consumed by mPIPE hardware, and others are consumed by Tile software.

Examples:

mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/ingress/app.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, and mpipe/multi_rules/app.c.

Field Documentation

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::notif_ring

Notification ring into which this packet descriptor is written.

Typically written by load balancer, but can be overridden by classification program if NR is asserted.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::channel

Source channel for this packet.

Written by mPIPE DMA hardware.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::__reserved_0

Reserved.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::me

MAC Error.

Generated by the MAC interface. Asserted if there was an overrun of the MAC's receive FIFO. This condition generally only occurs if the mPIPE clock is running too slowly.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::tr

Truncation Error.

Written by the iDMA hardware. Asserted if packet was truncated due to insufficient space in iPkt buffer

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::l2_size

Written by the iDMA hardware.

Indicates the number of bytes written to Tile memory. In general, this is the actual size of the packet as received from the MAC. But if the packet is truncated due to running out of buffers or due to the iPkt buffer filling up, then the L2_SIZE will be reduced to reflect the actual number of valid bytes written to Tile memory.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/ingress/app.c, and mpipe/loopback/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::ce

CRC Error.

Generated by the MAC. Asserted if MAC indicated an L2 CRC error or other L2 error (bad length etc.) on the packet.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::ct

Cut Through.

Written by the iDMA hardware. Asserted if packet was not completely received before being sent to classifier. L2_Size will indicate number of bytes received so far.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::bucket_id

Written by the classification program.

Used by the load balancer to select the ring into which this packet descriptor is written.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::__reserved_1

Reserved.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::cs

Checksum.

Written by classification program. When 1, the checksum engine will perform checksum based on the CSUM_SEED, CSUM_START, and CSUM_BYTES fields. The result will be placed in CSUM_VAL.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::nr

Notification Ring Select.

Written by the classification program. When 1, the NotifRingIDX is set by classification program rather than being set by load balancer.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::dest

Written by classification program.

Indicates whether packet and descriptor should both be dropped, both be delivered, or only the descriptor should be delivered.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::sq

General Purpose Sequence Number Enable.

Written by the classification program. When 1, the GP_SQN_SEL field contains the sequence number selector and the GP_SQN field will be replaced with the associated sequence number. When clear, the GP_SQN field is left intact and be used as "Custom" bytes.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::ts

TimeStamp Enable.

Enable TimeStamp insertion. When clear, timestamp field may be filled with custom data by classifier. When set, hardware inserts the timestamp when the start of packet is received from the MAC.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::ps

Packet Sequence Number Enable.

Enable PacketSQN insertion. When clear, PacketSQN field may be filled with custom data by classifier. When set, hardware inserts the packet sequence number when the packet descriptor is written to a notification ring.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::be

Buffer Error.

Written by the iDMA hardware. Asserted if iDMA ran out of buffers while writing the packet. Software must still return any buffer descriptors whose C field indicates a valid descriptor was consumed.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/ingress/app.c, and mpipe/loopback/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::ctr0

Written by the classification program.

The associated counter is incremented when the packet is sent.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::__reserved_2

Reserved.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::ctr1

Written by the classification program.

The associated counter is incremented when the packet is sent.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::__reserved_3

Reserved.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::csum_start

Written by classification program.

Indicates the start byte for checksum. Relative to 1st byte received from MAC.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::csum_seed_val

Checksum seed written by classification program.

Overwritten with resultant checksum if CS bit is asserted. The endianness of the CSUM value bits when viewed by Tile software match the packet byte order. That is, bits[7:0] of the resulting checksum value correspond to earlier (more significant) bytes in the packet. To avoid classifier software from having to byte swap the CSUM_SEED, the iDMA checksum engine byte swaps the classifier's result before seeding the checksum calculation. Thus, the CSUM_START byte of packet data is added to bits[15:8] of the CSUM_SEED field generated by the classifier. This byte swap will be visible to Tile software if the CS bit is clear.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::custom0

Written by the classification program.

Not interpreted by mPIPE hardware.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::custom1

Written by the classification program.

Not interpreted by mPIPE hardware.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::custom2

Written by the classification program.

Not interpreted by mPIPE hardware.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::custom3

Written by the classification program.

Not interpreted by mPIPE hardware.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::gp_sqn

Sequence number applied when packet is distributed.

Classifier selects which sequence number is to be applied by writing the 13-bit SQN-selector into this field. For devices that support EXT_SQN (as indicated in IDMA_INFO.EXT_SQN_SUPPORT), the GP_SQN can be extended to 32-bits via the IDMA_CTL.EXT_SQN register. In this case the PACKET_SQN will be reduced to 32 bits.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::packet_sqn

Written by notification hardware.

The packet sequence number is incremented for each packet that wasn't dropped.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::time_stamp_ns

Written by hardware when the start-of-packet is received by the mPIPE from the MAC.

This is the nanoseconds part of the packet timestamp.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::time_stamp_sec

Written by hardware when the start-of-packet is received by the mPIPE from the MAC.

This is the seconds part of the packet timestamp.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

int_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::va

Virtual address.

Must be sign extended by consumer.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::__reserved_4

Reserved.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::stack_idx

Index of the buffer stack to which this buffer belongs.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::__reserved_5

Reserved.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::inst

Instance ID.

For devices that support automatic buffer return between mPIPE instances, this field indicates the buffer owner. If the INST field does not match the mPIPE's instance number when a packet is egressed, buffers with HWB set will be returned to the other mPIPE instance. Note that not all devices support multi-mPIPE buffer return. The MPIPE_EDMA_INFO.REMOTE_BUFF_RTN_SUPPORT bit indicates whether the INST field in the buffer descriptor is populated by iDMA hardware.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::hwb

Always set to one by hardware in iDMA packet descriptors.

For eDMA, indicates whether the buffer will be released to the buffer stack manager. When 0, software is responsible for releasing the buffer.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::size

Encoded size of buffer.

Set by the ingress hardware for iDMA packet descriptors. For eDMA descriptors, indicates the buffer size if .c indicates a chained packet. If an eDMA descriptor is not chained and the .hwb bit is not set, this field is ignored and the size is specified by the .xfer_size field. 0 = 128 bytes 1 = 256 bytes 2 = 512 bytes 3 = 1024 bytes 4 = 1664 bytes 5 = 4096 bytes 6 = 10368 bytes 7 = 16384 bytes

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::c

Chaining configuration for the buffer.

Indicates that an ingress packet or egress command is chained across multiple buffers, with each buffer's size indicated by the .size field.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_PDESC_t::words[8]

Word access.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

The documentation for this union was generated from the following file:
  • arch/mpipe_shm.h
